what_it_does: A React modal component (assigned to window.SaveAsTypeModal) that lets a user review and edit a draft Type extracted by Vi's promoteInstance before committing it to CV_REGISTRY. It subscribes to window.CV_TYPES_PROMPT for the draft, exposes editable fields (name, id, layer, family, description) and an editable list of variables, and on Save calls window.CV_REGISTRY.register(draft).
observations: Uses global React via destructured useState/useEffect aliased as useState_sat/useEffect_sat.
Subscribes to window.CV_TYPES_PROMPT?.subscribe in an effect and returns the unsubscribe; renders null when no draft.
patch/patchVar/rmVar/addVar implement immutable updates to the draft and its variables array.
addVar seeds a new variable with key 'var_N', label 'New', default '', kind 'text'.
save() validates name and id are non-empty (toasts via window.dsaToast otherwise), then registers and toasts success.
Layer select is populated from window.CV_REGISTRY.LAYERS / LAYER_INFO; variable kind select offers text/number/url/choice.
Inline styles use design tokens (var(--font-body), var(--fg-muted), var(--accent-bronze)).
Renders ViShape and uses dsa-btn / tb-modal class conventions; copy notes variables 'become Template parameters when the Type is run.'
standouts: The whole modal is a thin review/edit gate over an AI-extracted draft (Vi's promoteInstance) before it enters the registry — human-in-the-loop promotion of an instance to a reusable Type.
Variables edited here are explicitly framed as the parameters the Type exposes when run as a Template.
conventions: Follows the project's single-registry pattern: registration goes through CV_REGISTRY.register, not a parallel store.
Uses design tokens for all styling values (no raw hex), consistent with the no-second-home rule.
Loud-ish failure via toasts on invalid input, though register() itself is wrapped only in try/finally (no catch).
Optional-chaining guards on globals (CV_TYPES_PROMPT, dsaToast) tolerate their absence.
concerns: save() wraps register in try/finally with no catch — if CV_REGISTRY.register throws, busy resets but the error is unhandled and the user gets no feedback, which sits uneasily with the project's loud-fail rule.
Layer select has no fallback if draft.layer is unset (controlled select with possibly undefined value).
notes: This is the human review step that turns a Vi-extracted instance draft into a registered, reusable Type via CV_REGISTRY.register.
questions: What produces the draft pushed through CV_TYPES_PROMPT, and what shape does promoteInstance emit (does it always include layer)?
Does CV_REGISTRY.register throw on duplicate id, and if so should save() surface that to the user?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header comment describes a small surface-anywhere modal wrapping promoteInstance's result in a review/edit step before committing to the registry, which is exactly what the component does.
apparent_themes: Interface as a projection that writes back into the registry
intention_signals: Trying to make Type creation a lightweight, reviewable act anywhere in the app — Vi proposes a Type from a concrete instance and the user confirms it into the one registry.
novelty: The pairing of an AI-extracted draft (promoteInstance) with an editable variables grid whose entries directly become the Type's runnable Template parameters.
contribution: Contributes the review/commit UI that bridges Vi's instance-to-Type extraction and CV_REGISTRY.register, including the inline editor for a Type's variables.
summary_for_embedding: A React modal that reviews and edits a Vi-extracted draft Type (name, id, layer, family, variables) and commits it to CV_REGISTRY via register.
intention_for_embedding: To let a user confirm and refine an AI-proposed Type before it enters the single registry, turning a concrete instance into a reusable, parameterised Template.
